content: Just as linguistic reflection (the double meaning of reflection as both light bouncing back and deliberate critical thinking) is reinforced through explicit vocabulary learning in high school language curricula, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by intentionally anchoring their self-critical feedback to explicit, lexically grounded memory traces of past failed outputs, rather than only abstract implicit self-evaluation. This bridges the lexical pedagogical understanding of reflection as a practiced, definable skill with the technical development of autonomous AI self-reflection.
